sub_pixel_system.notes: No sub-pixel movement system documented for NES port

special_mechanics.name: Maze Movement
special_mechanics.description: Pac-Man movement confined to enclosed maze. Speed increases as rounds progress. Ghosts have semi-random behavioral patterns.

physics_values.ai_behavior: semi-random with some pattern-based movement until first reversal

special_mechanics.name: Warp Tunnels
special_mechanics.description: Horizontal passages allowing Pac-Man to exit one side of maze and re-enter on opposite side. First three rounds have slowdown applied to ghosts in tunnels.

special_mechanics.name: Power Pellet / Energizer
special_mechanics.description: Larger pellets that cause ghosts to turn blue, become vulnerable, and allow Pac-Man to eat them for points. Duration decreases over rounds, eventually stopping.

feel_descriptors: pattern-breakable in original (less so in NES due to semi-random ghosts)
tier_notes: Tier 2: No precise physics values documented. Sources provide gameplay mechanics and design differences from original arcade, but no quantified movement parameters, gravity values, or sub-pixel data. NES port released November 1993 by Namco. Game mechanics emphasize maze traversal and ghost avoidance rather than precision platforming physics.
sources_used: The Cutting Room Floor (disambiguation page - limited specific technical data)
